failure_modes.text: Analysis paralysis can keep the team waiting for perfect information instead of learning through reversible movement.
failure_modes.mitigation: Set a decision deadline that forces commitment before analysis consumes more value than the option preserves; make the deadline visible and non-negotiable.

failure_modes.text: The paradox of choice can consume cognitive energy and leave the system selecting no option at all.
failure_modes.mitigation: Reduce cognitive load by pre-filtering: eliminate clearly dominated options early so the remaining set is small enough for genuine deliberation.

failure_modes.text: Anchoring on the first option presented can quietly shrink the perceived option set and bias later evaluation.
failure_modes.mitigation: Actively generate alternatives before evaluating: brainstorm options independently from the first one presented to prevent anchoring from shrinking the choice set.

premortem_questions.text: Is the worst case bad enough?
Would we regret making or not making this decision when we look back later?
premortem_questions.source_quote: one visualizes themselves at age 80 and asks if they would regret making (or not making) a specific decision

premortem_questions.text: Have we generated enough options before narrowing the choice set?
premortem_questions.source_quote: generate many options before selecting among them

heuristics.text: Use the 3+ principle to force more than a binary choice on important decisions.
Invent first and decide later so evaluation does not choke off option generation too early.
Bias toward action on reversible decisions because they buy information at low downside cost.

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready on anti-binary option generation, reversible decisions, and anti-paralysis discipline.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor-style portfolio overlays because the raw source is stronger on preserving viable paths and deciding after learning.
open_questions: The regret prompt is normalized from the Bezos example rather than preserved as a direct quote so it works as a reusable chunk.

allies: The raw allies section is strong on systems-thinking, critical-thinking, and hypothesis-driven option generation for later relation preservation.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section is stronger than any explicit tension block and points to confirmation, commitment/consistency, and availability-style narrowing.
structured_tensions: The source lacks an explicit structured tension block, so any later tension preservation should stay conservative and review-first.
